mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns are not statistically significant during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer spending and residential investment, which transmits to equity markets through lower earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Joint declines in real personal income and housing starts fail to predict negative returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term equity momentum through improved corporate output metrics.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration yields negative forward returns when consumer price index growth exceeds three percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and stronger consumer spending capacity, which mechanically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or zero over consecutive twelve-month windows despite consistent payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 270, inflationary pressures trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 70 percent of observations where the consumer price index exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal income confirms broad-based demand expansion, which reinforces corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.01 when financial stress indicators spike.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production combined with rising unemployment signals a supply-demand mismatch that forces corporate inventory drawdowns, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p-value > 0.10) across multiple market cycles.
